chapters.chapter_summary: The video begins with an introduction and transitions to an overview of the gallbladder's anatomy and function. The patient is positioned supine, and the surgical team prepares for laparoscopic cholecystectomy.
Three incision points are marked for the insertion of laparoscopic instruments near the navel and lower right side of the abdomen.
Surgical tools are inserted into the abdomen. One tool inflates the abdomen with gas, and others are used to prepare for the manipulation of organs.
Robotic arms manipulate tissues around the cystic duct, leading to the removal of the gallbladder.
Surgical suction is used to remove excess fluid post-gallbladder extraction.
The incisions are closed with sutures, followed by skin glue or skin closure tape.
The video concludes with a message about potential complications that may require open surgery if necessary.

SOAP.Subjective: I performed a laparoscopic cholecystectomy on a patient with gallstones and biliary dyskinesia. The patient was positioned supine and general anesthesia was administered.
SOAP.Objective: I observed the insertion of laparoscopic instruments through three incisions. The abdomen was inflated with carbon dioxide gas to facilitate visualization. I identified the gallbladder and its cystic duct, which were then clipped and the gallbladder was removed.
SOAP.Assessment: I assessed the procedure as successful without complications. The gallbladder was removed, and hemostasis was achieved.
SOAP.Plan: I closed the incisions with sutures and applied skin glue. The patient will be monitored for any postoperative complications.
